planner:
You are solving CayleyPy RapaportM2. The ONLY legal move tokens are I, S, K. Never invent H, L, X, Rk or any other symbols.
Design a constructive polynomial-time algorithm to sort an arbitrary permutation.
You may use the known trick: C = SK is an n-cycle with order evens increasing then odds decreasing; and conjugation P I P^{-1} swaps any adjacent pair in that cycle order.
Your plan must explicitly define: (1) how to build the cycle order, (2) how to implement swap(cycle[j-1],cycle[j]) as a sequence of I/S/K moves, (3) an insertion-sort style procedure that places correct values at indices cycle[0], cycle[1], ... while never breaking already-fixed positions.
No BFS/DFS/A*. Provide a short correctness argument.
coder:
Write a single self-contained solve_module.py for RapaportM2 using I, S, K only.
Contract: solve(vec)->(moves,sorted_array) and script mode prints JSON with keys moves,sorted_array.
Moves must be list[str] and every token must be exactly one of {I,S,K}.
Implement apply_I/apply_S/apply_K in-place.
Implement swap_adjacent_in_cycle(j) using P + [I] + reverse(P), where P represents C^{-j} and C=SK (so C^{-1}=KS).
Then insertion-sort along the cycle order to achieve the globally sorted array.
Do not emit any other move letters. Return only the full python file in one ```python``` block. Return exactly one complete Python file inside one ```python``` block. Do not add explanations outside the block. Inside the code, omit comments and docstrings unless they are truly necessary. Preserve the public solve(...) entrypoint and the script-mode JSON stdout contract.
fixer: Fix the RapaportM2 solver but keep the same constructive algorithm family (cycle order + conjugation swaps). Preserve the exact contract solve(vec)->(moves,sorted_array). Ensure every emitted move token is one of I/S/K and never emit H, L, X, Rk or any competition-specific moves from other puzzles. Return the full file only. Return exactly one complete Python file inside one ```python``` block. Do not add explanations outside the block. Inside the code, omit comments and docstrings unless they are truly necessary. Prefer a minimal patch over a rewrite while preserving the public solve(...) entrypoint and the script-mode JSON stdout contract.
